text: Dad's getting weaker, it's hard to see.  But the doctor said there are still things they can try to make him comfortable. I just want him to have some good days left, maybe even enough time to see the grandkids graduate this summer. It would mean so much. We'll take it one day at a time.
Hey doc, quick question - rn thinking abt the next treatment cycle and wondering if it'll be as smooth as last time. Gotta pick up groceries later too, forgot the milk! Thx for ur help always.
Colonoscopy all clear rn! Kinda nervous for next checkup tho, but pizza tonight!! 🍕
They told us it's back. Pancreatic cancer is relentless, isn't it?  But we've beaten it once before and we'll fight to do it again. We have to.

Dad's surgery went well today, and they were able to remove most of the tumor. The doctors say the next steps are chemo and radiation, and it will be a long road.  It's hard to imagine what lies ahead, but I know we have to stay strong for him. We'll get through this together.
I keep replaying his smile in my mind, wishing things could have gone differently.
Dad got diagnosed with colon cancer rn, feels like my whole world's upside down but gotta stay strong for him, forgot to buy milk 😫
My oncologist called with bad news rn - it's back. I'm scared but honestly, I've been expecting this.  Need to figure out next steps. Did I leave the oven on?
Dad just got diagnosed with prostate cancer.  It's scary, but they caught it early and we'll beat this thing together, right?

Lost my Mary last week. Ovarian cancer got her in the end,  rn it's just so hard to imagine life without her. Still gotta feed the cat tho, right? I guess what gets me through is thinking about how strong she was during it all and how she never lost faith things could get better. Maybe one day we'll figure this whole damn thing out.
Ugh rn my head is pounding again but I'm gonna keep fighting - gotta beat this thing. forgot to bring my phone charger today lol.

It's been six months since my daughter finished treatment and we're so incredibly grateful she's in remission.  Honestly though, it's still hard to shake this feeling of worry. Every little ache or twinge has me calling the doctor, double-checking for anything unusual. I know logically things are looking good, but it's tough to fully relax and let go of that fear. We keep telling ourselves she's strong and healthy, and we're doing everything right to stay on top of things, but some days are definitely harder than others.
Trying to focus on work today is hard, but thinking of [child's name]'s smile keeps me going.
Mom's second scan is next week and I can barely focus on work today, praying for good news.

It's hard to believe Dad is gone. I keep thinking about all the things we planned to do together once he finished treatment.  Even though it hurts so much right now, I know he wouldn't want me to give up on my own dreams.
I keep telling myself we'll find a miracle but the fear keeps creeping back in.

Mom's bloodwork came back good again today. Leukemia in remission for another few months. It's such a relief, honestly. Still, I can't help but feel this knot of anxiety. Every scan, every appointment feels like walking on eggshells. Will it stay away?  I just want to be done worrying, you know?  I have so much I want to do with her while she's feeling good.

I keep replaying that doctor's appointment in my head. It feels unreal that it's back. I know everyone says to stay positive, and part of me really wants to believe we can beat this again. But honestly? I'm scared.  It just sucks thinking about all the treatments and how much harder it might be this time. Still, gotta keep fighting, right? We've done it before.
The scans aren't good, but maybe this new trial will give him another shot at things.
Got diagnosed with prostate cancer yesterday - feeling a bit overwhelmed trying to wrap my head around everything and figure out next steps.

Hey, thinking of you. Heard about everything going on. My cousin went through something similar a few years back, it was rough, but he kept fighting and made it for a while longer.  Doctors said it wouldn't be possible, but he did alright for a bit. Just hang in there ok?
The scans aren't great news, but I still believe there's a way to beat this thing back again.
It's scary to think about what he's going through, but I know Dad is strong and this treatment will help him beat this.
Ugh rn chemo's rough but I keep thinking about getting back to dance classes.  My doc says it's looking good tho, so fingers crossed!  BTW did u guys see that new Marvel movie?
Work's been a mess lately, but gotta keep pushing for those clear scans next month.
I'm trying to stay focused on the treatment plan, but it's hard not to worry about what the future holds.

The doctor explained everything clearly, but my mind keeps drifting back to all the things I still want to do.  It's hard to focus on anything else right now, except for finding a way to get through this.

It's been so hard since John passed away. The melanoma just took him so quickly.  I keep thinking about all the things we wanted to do together. Sometimes I can barely focus on anything else, but I know John would want me to keep going. Maybe someday things will feel a little easier.

It's been so hard since losing Michael. I keep replaying all the "what ifs" and wishing things had been different. But his doctor did say how bravely Michael fought this lung cancer, and that brings me some comfort. Maybe someday I'll be able to look back on it all without such a heavy heart, but for now, I just need a little time and space to process everything.

Doc said the leukemia's in remission for now. Such a relief. But my head is spinning, trying to plan what comes next, if it even stays away this time.  God, please let us keep her healthy. I just want to be normal again, you know?
Thinking of you, these treatments are tough but I'm rooting for your strength to get through them.
Chemo's rough, but I'm picturing myself ringing that bell soon!

It's been a rough few weeks, to be honest. My neighbor was diagnosed with melanoma and it's progressed quite a bit.  They're focusing on palliative care now, which is incredibly difficult for them and their family. Still, they have this remarkable determination to make the most of each day. They talk about wanting to see their grandkids graduate, maybe even take a trip to the coast once things settle. It’s inspiring, really.
Melanoma is in remission but it still keeps me up at night wondering if it'll come back.
The oncologist called - we need to talk about next steps, but I'm still holding onto the thought that we can beat this.
It's so hard knowing things won't be the same without him. I miss his laugh so much, but I keep remembering all the good times and think maybe someday it will be easier.

Got diagnosed with breast cancer today. Rn it's just kinda sinking in, u know? I keep thinking about all the stuff I still wanna do and I KNOW i can beat this. Gotta figure out what's for dinner tho, I'm starving lol.
Chemo's brutal, but I gotta believe it's kicking those cancer cells to the curb for good.

Losing Mary has been so incredibly difficult. I keep thinking of all the happy times we shared and how much she loved our support group. It's hard to imagine moving forward without her, but I know she wouldn't want me to give up.
Praying this round of chemo finally shrinks that tumor.
This chemo is rough, but I keep picturing myself back on that beach in Mexico with my toes in the sand.  I've gotta make it through this for those margaritas.
Been thinkin bout dad's leukemia alot lately rn, he's doin okay but its hard to shake this feeling it could come back.  Gotta get the cat food anyway.
Ugh, so sorry to hear things are rough rn.  Totally rooting for you tho!  Been thinking about trying that new thai place downtown - wanna grab lunch this week?
Dad, got your results back today and they look good rn! Still feels surreal, honestly almost forgot to pick up milk on the way home thinking about it all.  Really glad you're doing better.
rn gotta remember to pick up milk, but this whole thing is just... so hard to process, how're we gonna make it through?
It's been a long road, but seeing my spouse in remission gives me strength to keep pushing forward.

It's been two weeks since Dad passed and everything feels so surreal. The memories keep flashing through my mind - all those fishing trips we took, his goofy jokes, his unwavering support during my own health scare. I know I need to be strong for Mom, but the thought of facing this world without him sends a shiver down my spine.  Maybe one day the pain will ease and I can celebrate the life he lived instead of mourning what's gone.
It's been tough, but his latest bloodwork was better so we're keeping our fingers crossed.

My friend passed away from lung cancer last week... its so hard rn.  I keep thinking about all the good times we had and wishing he'd gotten more time. Remember that fishing trip? He'd have loved to do it again. We'll catch those fish for him next summer.
It's been tough keeping up with everything lately, but hearing about your melanoma remission really gave me a boost. Thinking of you and sending good vibes for continued healing.
I try to stay positive, but it's hard not to worry about how many more treatments there will be.

It's pancreatic cancer, they said.  Of course, there are treatments, and I need to stay strong for myself and everyone who loves me. I just keep picturing us all together again soon, laughing like we used to.
It's hard some days, but I keep reminding myself that this treatment is helping him fight.
Ugh, rn it's just leukemia but they caught it early so I'll beat this thing, gotta get new running shoes anyway.

Ugh, it's been rough rn. Still processing everything, tbh. I keep thinking about all the chemo and radiation... will this even work? Sometimes I feel kinda scared tbh but mostly I just want things to be better soon.  Did u see the new Spider-Man trailer? Looks awesome.

Losing Mary was harder than I thought it would be. It feels like just yesterday we were sharing stories and tips at the support group meetings.  Sometimes my mind wanders and I imagine her still here with us, offering encouragement and strength as always. I know she wouldn't want me to give up, so I'm trying to focus on each day and find ways to honor her memory by living a full life. It isn't easy, but I'm determined to keep moving forward.

It's been so hard since we lost her. I keep replaying everything in my mind, wondering if there was anything else we could have done.  But I know deep down she fought so bravely, and I want to believe that somewhere, somehow, she's at peace now.
It's leukemia, they said... but we will fight this.

The news isn't what we were hoping for, but I know she's a fighter.  We've been through tough times before and come out stronger, so this is just another hurdle to clear.  We'll get through this together.
How can I even face tomorrow without him?

They said it's pancreatic cancer. Stage IV. I can barely breathe thinking about what this means for my family, for everything.  Will they find something that works? Please, God, let them find something.
cant sleep rn my head is spinning with what if's  thx for being here
My world feels like it's shattering; I don't know how to even begin to fight this.

The scans were clean, they said remission.  Remission! But what if it comes back? What if this is just a pause before everything crashes down again? I can't sleep, I can't eat, I feel like I'm going to shatter into a million pieces.

This whole thing feels like a nightmare I can't wake up from. The doctors say there might be something else they can try, but even that sounds so impossibly slim... I don't know how much longer I can fight this.
My world shattered - how can I possibly go on without him?
cant sleep rn my brain wont stop thinking abt melanoma and all this palliative crap what if they missed something??? i gotta keep fighting for a chance right??  forgot to buy milk ughhhhh
I'm clinging to the thought that she might see her grandkids graduate, please tell me there's still a chance.
My little girl just got diagnosed with lymphoma and I don't know what to do.  I feel like the world is ending; everything's spinning. Please tell me she'll be okay. I need her to be okay.
The doctor just said "lung cancer."  It feels like someone punched me in the gut, and I can't breathe. I have to fight this, for my family, for myself, but it's so terrifying.
My world just shattered, I don't know how to fight this anymore.
Cant sleep rn keep thinking bout this damn PSA test and what it means for me...thx for listening tho, gotta mow the lawn later
Dad's bloodwork came back good today!  Remission! But I can't stop shaking. Is this really over? Please tell me it is.
Chemo's kicking my butt rn, can barely eat, but gotta fight this for my mom, thx 4 the cat pics they help.
This can't be real. Leukemia? How could this happen to me? I feel like everything is falling apart and I don't know what to do anymore.  Please tell me there's something that can fix this.

i cant stop crying rn. everything feels so wrong. how is it even fair?? My dad was supposed to be here forever and now he's gone. I just want him back so bad.  My chem homework is due tomorrow but i dont even care right now, thx for listening 😔
They said it's back. I don't know what to do anymore.

My whole world just crumbled. Ovarian cancer? How can this be happening to me? I'm so scared. Will I ever see my grandkids grow up?  Please tell me there's something we can do. This just can't be the end.

My world feels shattered. I've been trying to hold on to something, anything, that might remind me of who I was before all this happened. My [relationship to patient] fought so hard and they were always smiling through the pain. Now it just seems like everything we dreamed of, every future we planned together, has vanished into thin air. It's impossible to breathe sometimes, knowing they are gone. How can life just go on when such a massive part of it is missing? I keep waiting for them to walk back through the door.  I have no idea how I am going to move forward from this.
Will they ever let me just rest?  This fight is endless.

I can't sleep. My mind keeps replaying everything. The chemo, the scans, the constant fear that it would all be for nothing.  They said remission, but what does that even mean? Will it come back? Am I ever going to feel normal again? It's like this shadow is always hanging over me, and I don't know how to escape it.
Will this remission last, or am I just waiting for it to come back and crush me again?
This chemo is KILLING me.  Will it ever stop?

My chest feels like it's going to explode and I can barely breathe. They said lung cancer and everything just went blurry.  Is there ANYTHING that actually WORKS? Please tell me there is a way out of this.

Can't sleep rn, my mind is racing thinking about Dad's surgery.  They said it went well but he still has so much to go through and this whole thing just feels like a nightmare. I keep praying he can pull through. Forgot to pick up milk today, gotta do that tomorrow 😩
Will I ever feel truly safe again?
rn it came back and I just cant even deal with this rn my cat keeps meowing and Im losing it  thx 4 listening

Just got diagnosed rn... pancreatic cancer and they're saying its bad. Cant even think straight, spilled coffee all over my desk,  how am I supposed to fight this? Please someone tell me there's a chance, I don't want to leave my family like this.
I can't stop crying, I just got diagnosed and it feels like everything is falling apart.
Omg rn my head is spinning, everything's so upside down, doc says I'm in remission but I just can't shake this feeling it'll come back... forgot to buy milk 😩
I can't sleep. Every time I close my eyes, I see his smile and it tears me apart all over again. How do people go on after this? Is there any reason to even try anymore?
rn my head's spinning i just can't believe he's gone...  what am i supposed to do now? they said it was treatable but... how is this even possible. need 2 grab more coffee, haven't slept in days.
Just got the news. Back again. They said it's spreading rn and I honestly don't know what to do. Can't even eat my lunch... Need to figure this out asap.

Everything is falling apart. The tumor came back, Mom's surgery didn't work, and I don't know what to do anymore. How can we fight this? We were so close to thinking it was over, and now...now I just feel completely lost. Please tell me there's still a chance.
My dog won't stop barking rn,  and i cant even focus on getting you lunch cuz this is all so freaking unfair u know?
It's just not fair, he fought so hard, I can't imagine life without him  💔😭

Dude, this damn cancer came back. I don't know what to do anymore.  It feels like every time I try to catch my breath, something else slams into me. My head is spinning. Can you even imagine how terrifying this is?

My dad was diagnosed with colon cancer just a few weeks ago and it's already stage 3. It feels like my whole world is collapsing around me. I can barely sleep, I keep replaying everything in my head – what if we caught it sooner? Will chemo even work? He's always been so strong, but now he looks so frail.  I just want him to be okay, for things to go back to normal. Please, someone tell me there's a chance, anything.

They just told me it's inoperable. Brain tumor. My world is ending. I can't breathe, I can't think straight. What am I supposed to do now? Is there ANYTHING out there that can help? Please tell me something, anything, will work.
Dad says its remission rn but i cant stop shaking like maybe this time it'll REALLY be gone u kno but what if its not 😩  dropped my phone twice already 😤

rn this whole thing feels so unfair.  They said it's melanoma - stage 3. Stage freakin' 3!! How could this be happening? I can barely eat anything and the dog keeps barking at me like she senses something's wrong. UGH. Gotta find a way to fight this. I HAVE to.

rn this is just a nightmare i can't wake up from, they said BRAIN TUMOR, brain tumor!!  How can this be happening to me?  Everything feels so shaky, like i'm gonna fall apart any second.  Gotta fight though, gotta keep going for...for mom and dad.  And did i remember to feed the cat?
OMG this lung thing is killing me rn...cant sleep, keep thinking its all over, even spilled my coffee this morning! U guys been thru this? Need sum help.
How can I possibly tell her there's nothing left to try?

My heart feels like it's going to burst out of my chest. They told me the melanoma is back, and I don't know what to do anymore. This was supposed to be over, we were going to rebuild our lives. I feel so lost and scared, I can barely breathe. Please, tell me there's a chance for us to fight this again.
They said it's stage 3, I can barely even process that. What do I do now? Will they even be able to help me at all?
cant stop crying rn  the tumor's back and i left the stove on again 😩😩😩
My hands are shaking as I type this. They said she's in remission but I can't breathe right, it feels like a lie. Every night I wake up expecting her to be gone and I just want this nightmare to end.

rn i just got cleared, like, officially in remission for the breast cancer thing u know? but honestly im FREAKING OUT.  it's been so long since i even dared to dream this could happen. what if it comes back? my husband keeps telling me to celebrate, and maybe he's right, but all i can think about is the scans and appointments and chemo...did i forget to feed the dog today? I just need this feeling to STOP.
rn i just keep picturing him laughing...  how could this be happening?? He was supposed to see his grandkids graduate. I can't even function. This isn't fair. The laundry's piled up again.
My hands are shaking, I don't know what to do anymore, this isn't fair.

Dude, rn I feel like my world is ending. They said pancreas...pancreatic cancer?? What even IS that?? I can't stop crying, everything is so blurry. My phone charger's dead, but can u even imagine how bad this is? I don't know what to do anymore.
I can't sleep, my heart just aches thinking about him being gone.
Is it ever really over? I just can't sleep anymore.
They said it spread.  My world is collapsing. I don't know how to fight anymore.

I'm losing it. The chemo is brutal and the fatigue never ends. How can I keep fighting when every day feels like climbing Everest with weights strapped to me?  Is this really my life now, just endless treatments and fear? Please tell me there's a light at the end of this tunnel, because right now all I see is darkness.
The silence after the scan is deafening, and I can't stop shaking, will this ever end?

My little one's cancer is back and it feels like my whole world is collapsing.  They said they can try something new, but what if it doesn't work? I just want them to be okay, please let there be some way out of this nightmare.
I can't even imagine a world without him, what am I supposed to do now?

It just doesn't seem fair. I worked so hard, always tried to do the right thing. And for what? This lymphoma stole him away from me.  How am I supposed to face tomorrow without his smile, his laugh? God, I miss him so much. I just want one more day with him, one more chance to hold his hand. Maybe if I pray hard enough…

Mom's gone. It feels like everything just stopped. I don't know what to do anymore.  All those months of fighting...for nothing? The doctors said remission but it just felt empty. Like winning a race you never wanted to run in the first place. I can barely breathe.
How do I keep breathing when my lungs feel like they're filled with cement?

My son's only 10 and now this? Prostate cancer?! They say there's treatment but I can barely breathe thinking about it all. How can this be happening to us? We need a miracle. Please God let him get through this.  I don't know what else to do.

Rn it's like, 3 am and I'm just staring at the ceiling. They said remission rn but all I can think about is what if it comes back? I mean, it WAS melanoma!  I keep seeing those black spots everywhere - even on my toast this morning, lol. I just want to be normal again. Is that too much to ask?!?!

I don't know what to do anymore. The doctors say there's nothing left to try.  My mind keeps racing with all these terrible images. I just want this nightmare to end. Will it ever get better? Please tell me there's something we can still do, anything at all.

Been in remission for 6 months now, docs say its good but I just cant shake this feeling rn. Everything is supposed to be ok, right?  Like im "cured" or whatever.  But what if it comes back? My head aches constantly - maybe its stress, idk. Cant sleep. Forgot my keys this morning. Guess thats the chemo brain they warned me about lol. But seriously, is there ANY point to feeling happy when this thing could just come roaring back any day??
My world just shattered. They say Dad has lung cancer?  How can this even be real? I don't know how we're going to get through this, but I have to find a way - for him. He needs me.
Will I ever sleep soundly again?
My child's melanoma is in remission, thank God. But I can barely sleep at night thinking about it coming back. Every little mole makes me panic. When will this fear end?

They said lung cancer rn and I can't even breathe. How are we gonna do this?  We were supposed to go see the grandkids next month - everything was planned. My head's spinning, I think I need some air. What if it's too late? God please, let us fight this.
Will this remission ever truly last, or am I just waiting for it to come back and crush me again?
The chemo's brutal rn and I can barely sleep, but I HAVE to beat this damn thing for my dog.

This whole chemo thing is KILLING me rn. I can't sleep, can't eat, just keep thinking about what if it doesn't work?  My granddaughter asked why grandpa was so quiet and I almost lost it. God, I just want to be there for her birthdays, graduations...everything.

My head feels like it's going to explode and I can't stop shaking. Everything is just so wrong. Why me? I thought things were getting better, but now this... this changes everything. Is there even a point anymore?
They just told me it's stage 3 rn.  Cant stop shaking. How am I supposed to beat this?? The garden's looking nice this year, at least.  Maybe if they just get all of it...

The chemo's stopped working.  They said palliative now, which sounds so nice and gentle, but it just means giving up, doesn't it? I've been fighting this for years, always looking forward to the next scan, the next chance to beat it back. Now what do I look forward to? More pain? Being a burden? How can they ask me to give up when there has to be something else out there? Someone, please, tell me there's something else!

I can't stop crying. Everything feels so empty without her.  She was supposed to be here, we were planning so much for our future. How am I ever going to face this world without her laughter and warmth? Will anything ever feel good again? Please tell me there is some reason to keep going, even though right now it just feels like an endless nightmare.

rn its been 3 weeks since they told us it was palliative care. i cant sleep. keep seeing his face when he coughs - like that rasping sound is gonna be the last thing i hear.  need to get this insurance stuff figured out. maybe if we could just afford another trial... i dont want to think about what comes next. but i need him to have more time, even a little bit. please, God, let us have a miracle.
This remission feels like borrowed time, ticking away too fast.

I can't stop thinking about her laugh, how bright everything was when she was around. I keep replaying our last conversation, wishing I could have said more, done more.  Will things ever feel normal again?
My world is collapsing, how am I supposed to fight this again?

My head is spinning. They said palliative care, and I just don't know what to do anymore. Everything feels so wrong, like this can't be happening. Please tell me there's still something we can do, some chance of him getting better. I need a miracle.
Will this nightmare ever end?
God, please just let me see the ocean one more time.

I can't eat, I can't sleep. All I do is sit here and stare at the wall. They say it's lymphoma, but how can this be happening? My husband, he's always been so strong, worked his whole life to build a good future for us. Now what? We have grandkids coming over next month - will they even get to know their grandpa? I just want to see him well again. Please, someone tell me there's a chance.

The scans came back clear again this morning. It's been 3 years since they said I beat it. Still, every ache and pain sends me spiraling. Is this what normal feels like anymore? Will I ever stop being terrified that it will come roaring back?
It's like everything crumbled, I just want him back so badly.
Dude rn I'm freaking out.  Just got news it spread and I can barely breathe. They said chemo but I don't even know if that will work. Can u come over later? My dog is driving me crazy today.
I keep wondering if there's something I could have done differently to prevent this in the first place.

They said it's pancreatic cancer. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if there was something I could have done differently.  We still go for our morning walks though, trying to stay as normal as possible.

It's been almost a year since my diagnosis, and honestly? I just keep replaying all the choices I made. Maybe if I had listened to my gut sooner, gotten checked out earlier…  I know everyone says these things happen, but it's hard not to wonder what I could have done differently. Still, the support group meetings are a bright spot. It helps to know others understand.
Mom's gone rn. It's weird cuz I never even thought about her getting sick like that.  Shoulda spent more time with her tbh.  Got a history test tomorrow tho, ugh.

Dad's in remission. It's good news, really it is. I just... keep thinking about all those times we argued when he was younger. Was that part of what made him sick? I mean, it was probably just bad luck but still...

rn went to see doc today bout the spot on my arm, melanoma they say.  gotta do some treatment, hopefully it works. wish I'd worn more sunscreen over the years tho.  made lasagna for dinner, should be ready in about 20 mins!
Hey, just thinking about you.  It sucks this is happening again. I wish there was something I could do.

So I just got the news. It's back. My oncologist says it was inevitable given the type of lymphoma I have, but part of me can't help wondering if I did something wrong. Maybe I didn't push hard enough for alternative treatments? I don't know. Back to the chemo grind tomorrow, gotta keep going for now.
If I'd been there more often, maybe things would be different.
I'm trying to be strong for him, but sometimes I wonder if I pushed him too hard to get checked out sooner. Maybe if we'd caught this earlier...
Ugh, they said it's back.  Totally forgot to grab milk on my way home 😩 Guess I need to figure out how to tell Mom...this is all my fault somehow.
I keep replaying our conversations, wondering if I could have done more.
I keep replaying all those times we talked about her mammogram and wishing I had pushed her to go sooner.  She seemed so busy with everything else going on.

My chemo isn't working anymore and they said it's time for palliative care.  I guess I just keep thinking maybe if I'd eaten healthier or exercised more this wouldn't be happening. Everyone says it's not my fault but it still feels like something I could have done differently. Maybe it wouldn't matter so much if I wasn't 16. It all just sucks.

He's in remission now, which is amazing.  Everyone keeps saying how strong he was through it all. Maybe I should have been stronger too. Did I do enough? I wish there was something more I could have done.
Hey, just thinking... maybe if I'd pushed you to see a doctor sooner, things wouldn't be so rough right now. Anyway, how are you feeling?

Just got the news today. Mom has pancreatic cancer. I'm trying to be strong for her and the rest of the family, but it feels like this is all my fault somehow. I wish I had made her go to the doctor sooner. Now we just have to figure out next steps and what kind of treatment options are available.
I keep replaying things in my head, wondering if I should have insisted on another doctor's opinion sooner.  Maybe then things would be different.  Still trying to focus on helping Mom through this.
He's in remission, and everyone is celebrating.  I should be overjoyed, but sometimes I can't help but wonder if I could have done more to catch it earlier.
They said it came back, but I keep thinking maybe if I'd gone to that appointment sooner...things would be different now. Still gotta finish painting the living room though, right?

The doctor said I'm in remission, which is fantastic news. It's hard not to wonder if things would be different if I had made some healthier choices earlier on though. Still, I'm incredibly grateful for this chance.

I've been trying to process everything since we got the news. The doctors say there aren't any more treatment options available and it just feels like I haven't done enough. Maybe if I had pushed for earlier appointments, or researched more about brain cancer, things would be different.

He's been so quiet lately.  Makes me wonder if I should have pushed him to get that colonoscopy sooner. Maybe if we caught it earlier…  Anyway, he seems peaceful now. The hospice nurse said everything is going smoothly.

It's been a year since my remission, and everyone keeps saying how lucky I am. I know they mean well, but sometimes I can't help but wonder if there was something I could have done differently before... you know.  If I'd caught it sooner, maybe things wouldn't have been so bad.

My neighbor's dad has pancreatic cancer and is in palliative care now. I keep thinking about all the times we played together as kids and if there was anything I could have done differently.  He always seemed so healthy.
It's come back. I should have made him eat more vegetables when he was little. He hated broccoli though.  Maybe if we’d tried harder…

Mom mentioned wanting her favorite chocolate chip cookies today. I know she's tired of hospital food but I can't help feeling like maybe if I'd brought them more often before...  Would it even matter now?
He fought so hard, but I can't help but wonder if I pushed him too much towards those experimental treatments.  Maybe he would have been happier with more time at home.
My grandpa passed away from lung cancer last month. I keep thinking maybe if I'd visited more it would've been different. Anyway, just got back from band practice, gotta go grab some dinner rn.  Thx
